message: Hello! I'm your BPMN Modeling Agent. I'll analyze the task now and start planning the diagram.
I've analyzed the task and defined 7 goals for the model.

Iteration 1 — setting up the participant structure (pools/lanes) first. Building Authority is expanded with internal roles; Citizen and Environmental Agency are collapsed participants that interact via messages.

I’ll now model the full Building Authority process with the required message interactions (Citizen submission, Environmental Agency review) and the internal decisions, deadline wait, and final notifications.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 11 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 6 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 1/3…
Applying critical wiring fixes (missing outgoing branches) and adding clear internal termination EndEvents for the Building Authority outcomes.

I’ll fix Goal 1 by introducing clear internal (non-message) end states for each outcome, and converting the existing message EndEvents into separate message notifications (IntermediateThrowEvents) before those internal EndEvents. This keeps notifications while ensuring the internal process terminates cleanly and consistently.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 7 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 5 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 2/3…
Adding explicit citizen-notification message steps before every Building Authority end outcome (permit issued + all rejection branches), while keeping the internal EndEvents as plain termination.

I will add explicit citizen-notification message steps (to the collapsed PoolCitizen) before every Building Authority end outcome (permit issued + all rejection branches), keeping the existing EndEvents as the clear internal termination points.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 10 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 4 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 3/3…
Fixing the 4 critical structural errors: remove the invalid task-splits by routing each outcome through its notification step, then to the appropriate end event (no gateway needed).
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 5 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ After 3 correction attempt(s), 1 critical issue(s) remain. Please review manually.

Simplify the process. Delete the Notify results and if necessary the reject applications and create instead a SendTask with concrete definitions. Instead "Notify result" it could be "Send Rejection." Better would be to use EndEvents that sending the message then you don't need the Send Task before also.
I’ll simplify the model by removing the generic notification steps and using clearer, concrete send actions (and/or message end events) for each outcome.

I’ll simplify the outcome handling by removing the generic 'Notify result' send tasks and replacing them with explicit Message EndEvents for each outcome (send permit / send rejection). I’ll also remove the separate internal 'Reject application' user tasks so the branches terminate cleanly and directly at the message end events. Then I’ll reconnect the Citizen message flows to these new end events.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 9 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 5 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 1/3…

I’ll fix the 5 critical dead/unreached elements by wiring the intended success path through 'Issue permit' and by removing the unused plain EndEvents (keeping the message EndEvents as the actual terminations that also notify the Citizen).
I’ll resolve the critical issue by removing the unused/unreached plain EndEvent (EndPermitIssuedBA) and keep the message end event (EndPermitSentMsg) as the single termination for the approval path.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 8 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 2 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 2/3…

I’ll apply the two remaining critical fixes: (1) remove the invalid duplicate/ambiguous 'Yes' branch from GwTechOk by keeping only the path via 'Issue permit'; (2) fix the invalid process intake by turning 'CatchApplication' into the Message StartEvent (and removing the old none StartEvent + its sequence flow).
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 5 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 1 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 3/3…
Verified PoolBuildingAuthority has exactly one StartEvent (CatchApplication) and no sequence flow drives the intake. No changes needed for this goal based on the provided bpmn_model snapshot.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 4 issue(s) — details below.
